data.title: Modular blockchain Sophon: plans to launch SOPH token in May
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Modular blockchain Sophon announced on the X platform that SOPH tokens will be issued in May, and information such as token economics and community distribution will be shared soon.

data.title: The initial forecast for the US one-year inflation rate in May was 7.3%, the expected value was 6.5%, and the previous value was 6.5%
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: The initial value of the expected one-year inflation rate in the United States in May is 7.3%, the expected value is 6.50%, and the previous value is 6.50%.
The initial value of the expected five- to ten-year inflation rate in the United States in May is 4.6%, the expected value is 4.40%, and the previous value is 4.40%.
The initial value of the University of Michigan Consumer Confidence Index in the United States in May is 50.8, the expected value is 53.4, and the previous value is 52.2. (Jinshi)

data.title: Analysis: Binance Alpha Token Drives BNB Chain Activity, Weekly Volume Increases Over 114%
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Binance Alpha has significantly improved the on-chain activity of BNB Chain. Data shows that BNB Chain's weekly transaction volume has increased by 114.6%, with more than 1 million new addresses added daily. Of the first batch of Alpha tokens launched, 71% chose to be deployed on BNB Chain, 60% were released through the Initial DEX Offering (IDO) module of Binance Wallet, and the rest were launched through Launchpad and Launchpool. Among the top 20 Alpha tokens in terms of current trading volume, 9 are native to BNB Chain, including BSquared Network (B2), OKZOO (AIOT), Polyhedra Network (ZKJ), etc. In addition, more than 50% of the liquidity of some projects such as Haedal Protocol and Polyhedra Network has been deployed on BNB Chain, driving the growth of the total locked value (TVL) of the chain. Binance also further incentivized users to participate in Alpha token trading through the Binance Alpha Points event and trading competitions, which strengthened the demand for BNB. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: U.S. Inflation and Consumer Confidence Data for May Released
data.description: According to Odaily, the preliminary estimate for the United States' one-year inflation rate in May stands at 7.3%, surpassing the expected 6.50% and the previous value of 6.50%. Additionally, the five to ten-year inflation rate forecast for May is initially estimated at 4.6%, higher than the anticipated 4.40% and the prior figure of 4.40%. Meanwhile, the University of Michigan's preliminary consumer confidence index for May is reported at 50.8, falling short of the expected 53.4 and the previous value of 52.2.

data.title: Fidelity: BTC acceleration phase is still continuing, and will continue to set new highs before the end of this cycle
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Fidelity Digital Assets posted on the X platform that after 69 days of low profits and high volatility, Bitcoin's acceleration phase continues. This trend strengthens the Fidelity team's argument that Bitcoin may hit a new all-time high before the end of this cycle.

data.title: Believe founder: System issues are being fixed and creators will receive fees as planned
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Believe founder Ben Pasternak posted on the X platform, "I've seen a lot of concerns about handling fees, and there will be more explanations later. In short: we are receiving a large amount of transactions, which has caused problems in many systems, and we are fixing it. I have seen many error messages related to handling fees on the X platform. All creators will receive the fees as planned, and the repair may take a little time."

data.title: U.S. Mining Firm's Bitcoin Purchase Plan Stalls
data.description: According to Foresight News, HODL15Capital has revealed that despite the announcement by U.S. mining company Critical Metal Corps in January to purchase $500 million worth of Bitcoin, there have been no acquisitions or updates since then.

data.title: Bitcoin's Accelerated Phase Continues Amid Market Volatility
data.description: According to Odaily, Fidelity Digital Assets recently stated on the X platform that Bitcoin's accelerated phase is ongoing after experiencing 69 days of low profitability and high volatility. This trend supports Fidelity's argument that Bitcoin may reach a new all-time high before the end of the current cycle.

data.title: Ethereum researcher: Compared with Bitcoin, launching a 51% attack on Ethereum is more difficult and costly
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Ethereum researcher Justin Drake said that the cost of launching a 51% attack on Bitcoin is about $10 billion, which is much lower than the cost of attacking Ethereum. He pointed out that Ethereum's PoS mechanism not only increases the cost of attack, but also provides additional security through the community's social coordination mechanism. Justin Drake emphasized that the Ethereum community can identify and punish attackers, which is an advantage that the PoW mechanism does not have. In addition, Matan Sitbon, founder of Lightblocks, a blockchain interoperability developer, also said that the ultimate security of Ethereum depends not only on cryptography or protocol rules, but also on the community's strong social and economic coordination mechanisms. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Public funds in 14 U.S. states hold $632 million in MSTR exposure
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Julian Fahrer, founder of Bitcoin Laws, disclosed that 14 states in the United States reported that their public retirement funds and fiscal funds had MSTR exposure of $632 million in the first quarter. In the first quarter, the total increase was about $302 million, and the average holding size increased by 44%.

data.title: Believe Founder Addresses Concerns Over Transaction Fees
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Believe founder Ben Pasternak addressed concerns on social media regarding transaction fees. He acknowledged the high volume of transactions causing issues in several systems and assured that efforts are underway to resolve these problems. Pasternak noted that there is misinformation circulating about fees on platform X, but confirmed that all creators will receive their fees as planned, although the resolution may take some time.

data.title: Grayscale transferred 19,632 ETH, worth about $51.15 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to Arkham monitoring, about 2 minutes ago, Grayscale transferred a total of 19,632 ETH, worth approximately US$51.15 million, to two unknown addresses and the Coinbase Prime hot wallet address through its Ethereum exchange-traded fund.

data.title: U.S. States Increase Investment in MSTR Holdings in Early 2025
data.description: According to Foresight News, Julian Fahrer, founder of Bitcoin Laws, has revealed that public retirement and fiscal funds in 14 U.S. states have increased their exposure to MSTR holdings to $632 million in the first quarter of 2025. The states collectively added $302 million in MSTR investments during this period, marking an average increase of 44% in their holdings.

data.title: Ethereum's Security Compared to Bitcoin: Insights from Justin Drake
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Ethereum Merge architect Justin Drake has expressed concerns about the relative security of Bitcoin compared to Ethereum. Drake, who played a pivotal role in Ethereum's transition to a proof-of-stake (PoS) system, suggested that launching a 51% attack on Bitcoin would be more cost-effective than on Ethereum. He estimated that such an attack on Bitcoin could cost around $10 billion. This perspective aligns with a previous statement by Grant Hummer, co-founder of Etherealize, who claimed that Bitcoin's security budget is insufficient. Hummer estimated that a successful 51% attack on Bitcoin could cost $8 billion, potentially decreasing to $2 billion in the future. A 51% attack occurs when an entity gains control over more than half of a blockchain network's mining or staking power, allowing them to manipulate the network.

Drake elaborated on the challenges of executing a 51% attack on Ethereum, noting that achieving 100% control would require more than half of the staked Ether. At the time of his comments, there were over 34 million staked Ether, valued at nearly $89.6 billion. This means that acquiring the necessary stake would cost approximately $44.8 billion. Given Ethereum's market cap of $316 billion and a 24-hour trading volume of $25 billion, the financial requirements for such an attack are substantial. Moreover, any attempt to accumulate this amount of Ether would likely drive up its price, further increasing the cost of the attack.

Matan Sitbon, CEO of Lightblocks, highlighted Ethereum's additional security measures, emphasizing the role of the community's social and economic coordination mechanisms. Drake also pointed out that Ethereum's PoS system allows the community to identify and penalize attackers through social consensus, a feature not available in Bitcoin's proof-of-work (PoW) system. This social layer, comprising the network's human participants, can decide on software changes to counteract attacks. Pavel Yashin from P2P.org mentioned that if centralization is detected, the community could implement a new fork, rendering the compromised chain obsolete. Hassan Khan, CEO of Ordeez, added that while a 51% attack is theoretically possible, practical barriers make it highly improbable for both Bitcoin and Ethereum. He noted that Bitcoin's PoW system requires significant computing power and energy, while Ethereum's PoS introduces economic and governance challenges that deter such attacks.

data.title: LIBRA case judge orders full disclosure of Javier Milei bank accounts
data.description:
A judge ordered the bank secrecy requirements of Javier Milei and his sister to be lifted as part of the LIBRA fraud case.
source: https://protos.com/libra-case-judge-orders-full-disclosure-of-javier-milei-bank-accounts/

data.title: Cumberland transfers 1,022 BTC to Coinbase Institutional
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to Whale Alert monitoring, about 13 minutes ago, Cumberland transferred 1,022 BTC to Coinbase Institutional, worth more than US$105 million.

data.title: Bitcoin Bullish Sentiment Rises as Call Options Volatility Surpasses Puts
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Glassnode has reported that the implied volatility of Bitcoin call options has surpassed that of put options. This shift indicates a growing bullish sentiment in the market, with traders showing a preference for betting on price increases rather than hedging against potential declines. This trend is seen as a risk appetite signal supporting a potential Bitcoin price rally.

data.title: A meme coin themed after Trump's second son Eric Trump is suspected to be a scam. Beware of Rug Pull risks
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: A meme coin "ERICTRUMP" themed after Eric Trump, the second son of Trump, appeared on the Solana Ecosystem Meme Coin Launch Platform Pump.fun. It has risen by more than 6,200% in the past 24 hours, which has aroused the vigilance of the crypto community. Blockchain data platform Bubblemaps published an article on the X platform, saying that it is very likely to be a scam to beware of the risk of rug pull.
Rug pull usually refers to the sudden withdrawal of liquidity or large-scale sell-off by token insiders, which often leads to a sharp drop in prices, making the tokens held by retail investors worthless. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: OpenAI to Host Live Stream on Research Preview
data.description: According to PANews, OpenAI announced via its official X account that it will conduct a live stream in two hours. The content of the stream is hinted to be a 'low-key research preview.'

data.title: Apple expected to unveil AI roadmap in coming weeks, China will be key
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Wedbush analysts said in a research report that after Apple has dealt with most of the supply chain disruptions caused by the tariff war, it can now focus on announcing an artificial intelligence strategic roadmap that will lead to a "renaissance of growth." Apple is expected to announce Alibaba as its artificial intelligence partner in China before the Apple Developer Conference in June. This will lay the foundation for the global artificial intelligence strategy for the coming year, and China will be a key part of the growth puzzle. At the press conference, investors should hear more about the features Apple plans to launch and more news about Apple Intelligence. (Jinshi)
